rationale: Title X is more polished, concise, and clinically framed for JICE. It clearly states the complication, procedural setting, outcomes, and prognosis without overloading the reader. Title Y gives useful study specifics, including dry pericardiocentesis, sample size, and design, but reads more like a methods line than a journal title.
loser_weakness: Title Y is too long and crowded. The placement of “after dry pericardiocentesis in 86 ventricular arrhythmia patients” is awkward, and the title loses appeal despite strong specificity.
